title: Someone Went Over Niagara Falls and Lived
script: Someone went over Niagara Falls with no protection at all, and unbelievably survived. The person went over the edge where millions of gallons crash down every second onto jagged rocks below. The drop is nearly two hundred feet, and the force at the bottom is strong enough to crush a boat, yet rescuers found the person alive in the churning water and pulled them out. Only a tiny handful of people in history have ever survived the falls without a barrel or a suit, and most who go over do not. Experts still can't fully explain how the body absorbed that much force. So what makes the difference between surviving and not?
